Generate appropriate values for the resource parameter in get_auth_token

generate_resource.Rd

A helper function to generate appropriate values. Ensure that the version argument matches the aad_version argument to get_auth_token. It's unlikely that you will ever want to set authorise to FALSE but it's here as an option since AzureAuth::get_azure_token supports it. Similarly, you are likely to want to keep refresh turned on (this argument has no effect on v1 tokens, it only applies to v2).

Usage

generate_resource(
  version = 1,
  url = "https://storage.azure.com",
  path = "/.default",
  authorise = TRUE,
  refresh = TRUE
)

Arguments

version

numeric. The AAD version, either 1 or 2 (1 by default)

url

The URL of the Azure resource host

path

For v2, the path designating the access scope

authorise

Boolean, whether to return a token with authorisation scope, (TRUE, the default) or one that just provides authentication. You are unlikely to want to turn this off

refresh

Boolean, applies to v2 tokens only, whether to return a token that has a refresh token also supplied.

Value

A scalar character, or (in most v2 situations) a character vector

On this page
